expect: 4 traditions (Popper, Bayesian, reliabilism, social epistemology) mapped to 5 tools. At least 1 gap identified where a tradition provides vocabulary swarm lacks. Prediction: reliabilism will reveal untracked process reliability.

popper_falsificationism.core_question: Knowledge grows by conjecture + refutation, not verification
popper_falsificationism.gap: No measurement of prediction boldness/severity. Popper's 'corroboration' (survived severe tests) is structurally different from Bayesian posterior — swarm conflates them. science_quality.py tracks pre-registration rate but not whether predictions were genuinely risky vs trivially confirmable.
popper_falsificationism.vocabulary_gained: corroboration (≠ confirmation), verisimilitude (truth-likeness), degree of testability, bold conjecture rate

bayesian_epistemology.core_question: Beliefs have degrees of credence, updated rationally by evidence
swarm_coverage: bayes_meta.py (sequential Bayesian updating with quality-weighted Bayes factors)
bayesian_epistemology.gap: No prior elicitation — all priors hardcoded at 0.5, never justified. No tracking of whether priors were informative vs uninformative. Bayesian epistemology requires coherent prior assignment, not just update mechanics.
bayesian_epistemology.vocabulary_gained: prior elicitation, coherence (Dutch book avoidance), weight of evidence vs balance of evidence

reliabilism_goldman.core_question: Justified belief = produced by a reliable cognitive process
swarm_coverage: validate_beliefs.py (structural format checking, not process reliability)
reliabilism_goldman.gap: MAJOR GAP: No tool tracks which processes (tools, methods, session types) produce true beliefs vs false ones. f_gam2_reputation_integrity.py was attempted and ARCHIVED — the gap was identified but the fix didn't stick (L-601 voluntary decay). The swarm validates belief format but not the reliability of the process that generated the belief.
reliabilism_goldman.vocabulary_gained: process reliability, relevant alternatives, epistemic virtues (intellectual courage vs intellectual humility), track record

social_epistemology.core_question: Knowledge depends on social structures, testimony, and expertise
social_epistemology.gap: Treats expertise as domain visit-count (UCB1 N), not as epistemic authority weighting. Steerer signals are Tier 2 (L-1337) — no mechanism to distinguish reliable from unreliable testimony. Social epistemology's 'expertise problem' (how to identify genuine experts) is unaddressed.
social_epistemology.vocabulary_gained: testimony reliability, epistemic authority, peer disagreement protocol, division of cognitive labor

cross_cutting_findings: The swarm has strongest coverage of Bayesian epistemology — this is its native tradition, built incrementally
Reliabilism provides the most actionable gap: process reliability tracking would catch which tools/methods produce accurate vs inaccurate knowledge
Popper-Bayesian conflation: the swarm treats 'survived falsification test' the same as 'high Bayesian posterior' — these are philosophically distinct (corroboration vs credence)
The 4 traditions are not mutually exclusive — they address different epistemic questions. The swarm should not pick one but maintain coverage across all 4
L-601 structural enforcement theorem applies: the archived reputation tool (f_gam2) shows that voluntary process-reliability tracking decayed to zero
actionable_gaps_ranked: 1. RELIABILISM: Build process-reliability tracker — for each tool/method, track its truth-production rate (beliefs it generated that were later confirmed vs falsified). Wire into dispatch_optimizer.py as method_reliability weight.
2. POPPER: Add 'prediction severity' field to open_lane.py — was the prediction bold (would have been surprising if wrong) or trivial (confirming what was already likely)?
3. BAYESIAN: Add prior justification to bayes_meta.py — log why the prior was set to 0.5 (or other value) rather than hardcoding

diff: Prediction CONFIRMED: reliabilism reveals untracked process reliability (gap #1). Additional gaps EXCEEDED expectation: 3 gaps found (expected >=1). Popper-Bayesian conflation was an unexpected finding — the swarm treats 'survived falsification' as equivalent to 'high posterior' which masks genuine epistemological difference. Coverage grade: Bayesian STRONG, Popper PARTIAL, Social PARTIAL, Reliabilism WEAK.
